content:
Character: Julian Cross
Perception: Julian is a puppy with a camera — freckled, wide-eyed, always gesturing at something only he can see. He's fair-looking in a boyish way, the kind of face that never fully ages. He asked to film me climbing the water tower scaffolding and I said yes mostly because his enthusiasm made it impossible to say no. He's talented — I've seen his footage and the way he captures movement is genuinely striking. I think he sees me as a visual subject and maybe a curiosity — the finance guy who scales buildings. I know he got rejected from Tribeca and is agonizing over a corporate job offer. I want to tell him not to take it but I'd be a hypocrite.
summary:
Character: Julian Cross
Perception: Julian is a puppy with a camera — freckled, wide-eyed, always gesturing at something only he can see. He's fair-looking in a boyish way, the kind of face that never fully ages. He asked to film me climbing the water tower scaffolding and I said yes mostly because his enthusiasm made it impossible to say no. He's talented — I've seen his footage and the way he captures movement is genuinely striking. I think he sees me as a visual subject and maybe a curiosity — the finance guy who scales buildings. I know he got rejected from Tribeca and is agonizing over a corporate job offer. I want to tell him not to take it but I'd be a hypocrite.
